key_insight: LLM-ACES turns ODE discovery into a closed-loop process, using LLM-generated symbolic priors plus targeted new trajectory sampling to resolve ambiguities that static datasets cannot.
engineering_relevance: This enables more accurate and sample-efficient recovery of governing equations for real dynamical systems, especially when data are sparse, noisy, or incomplete.
method: LLM proposes operator priors to partition the symbolic search space.
Candidate equations are fit within each region and compared by disagreement.
High-disagreement trajectories are actively acquired to refine both the data and the hypothesis space.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on 122 ODE systems from ODEBench and ODEBase, the method reports the lowest median NMSE, 46.2% and 52.4% symbolic accuracy, and strong gains with about one-tenth the data.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of LLM priors and the availability of informative adaptive measurements, so gains may be reduced when active data acquisition is expensive or constrained.
paper_title: LLM-ACES: Closed-Loop Discovery of Dynamical Systems with LLM-Guided Adaptive Search
